task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged into the Bahmni EMR system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bahmni home screen or dashboard is visible, indicating a successful login (user 'superman' may be visible in the UI).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the correct patient record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The clinical dashboard or patient summary screen is open for patient 'Amara Okonkwo' with identifier 'BAH000002'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the disposition or consultation entry section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The consultation, disposition, or clinical entry tab/form is actively displayed on the screen for the patient.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selected the 'Admit Patient' disposition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown, radio button, or selection field for 'Disposition' clearly shows 'Admit Patient' as the selected value.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the required clinical notes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box or notes field contains the required text, specifically including the keywords: 'pneumonia', 'SpO2', '88%', 'IV antibiotics', and 'oxygen'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser GUI to interact with Bahmni.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is clicking and typing within a web browser displaying the Bahmni interface, rather than using a terminal, cURL, or API testing tool like Postman.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Bahmni EMR application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the authentic Bahmni EMR user interface, not a text editor, a mock HTML page, or a local document.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the UI using backend scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no visible terminal windows executing SQL commands, Python scripts, or database injections to alter the patient's record directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Identifier: BAH000002. Patient Name: Amara Okonkwo. Expected Disposition: 'Admit Patient'. Required Note Keywords: 'pneumonia', 'SpO2', '88%', 'IV antibiotics', 'oxygen'. The task must be performed in the Bahmni EMR system via its web interface.
